deep_dive.title: SAFETY SENTRY: Context-Aware Human Intervention via EXECUTE-ASK-REFUSE Routing
deep_dive.abstract: LLM agents act on real-world environments through tool calls, and a single misjudged action can cause irreversible harm. The standard safeguard is a guard model that labels each proposed action as safe or unsafe, but this binary view conflates two distinct decisions: whether the action is harmful in itself, and whether it is appropriate given the user's context. It also operates at the granularity of action categories rather than individual instances, producing routine interruptions that erode autonomy and train users to wave through the most consequential alerts. We reframe the problem as a per-instance three-way routing decision over {EXECUTE, ASK, REFUSE} and instantiate it with Safety Sentry, a lightweight guard model whose inference reduces to a single decoding call. A single decoding-time threshold lets one fixed checkpoint be re-positioned across deployments of differing risk tolerance without retraining. Safety Sentry outperforms a broad set of open-weight and frontier closed-source baselines on overall accuracy and safety-related recall, while controlling both directional error rates simultaneously.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: lightweight guard model with single decoding call for LLM action safety routing
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 轻量级防护模型单次解码实现LLM动作安全路由
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 轻量级防护模型单次解码实现LLM动作安全路由
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 轻量级防护模型单次解码实现LLM动作安全路由
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Existing binary guard models for LLM agents conflate harm and context-appropriateness, causing over-interruptions. Safety Sentry reframes safety as per-instance EXECUTE-ASK-REFUSE routing via a lightweight guard model requiring only a single decoding call, outperforming larger baselines on accuracy and safety recall.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM智能体防护模型采用二元安全分类，混淆了动作本身危害性与上下文适宜性，导致频繁误报和用户自主性受损。本文提出Safety Sentry，将安全问题重构为逐实例的三路路由决策{执行、询问、拒绝}，并实例化为一个轻量级防护模型，其推理仅需单次解码调用，且可通过单一阈值适配不同风险容忍度而无需重训练。实验表明，该模型在整体准确率和安全相关召回率上优于多种开源和闭源基线，同时有效控制双向错误率。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有LLM智能体防护模型采用二元安全分类，混淆了动作本身危害性与上下文适宜性，导致频繁误报和用户自主性受损。本文提出Safety Sentry，将安全问题重构为逐实例的三路路由决策{执行、询问、拒绝}，并实例化为一个轻量级防护模型，其推理仅需单次解码调用，且可通过单一阈值适配不同风险容忍度而无需重训练。实验表明，该模型在整体准确率和安全相关召回率上优于多种开源和闭源基线，同时有效控制双向错误率。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: compact efficient guard model for LLM input and output safety classification

quick_skim.title: HyperSafe: Inference-Time Safety Recovery for Fine-Tuned Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Safety alignment in large language models can be fragile under fine-tuning, as even benign task adaptation may increase harmful compliance. Existing defenses mainly follow two directions: they either intervene during or after fine-tuning through retraining or weight modification, which can be costly and may hurt task performance, or they use model-agnostic safety classifiers, which may miss failures specific to a given fine-tuned checkpoint. These limitations motivate a post hoc, model-specific, and non-invasive approach to safety restoration. To meet these requirements, we propose HyperSafe, a framework that restores safety behavior by generating a model-specific Safe Side Network (SSN) for each fine-tuned checkpoint. HyperSafe uses layer-wise activation fingerprints to capture how fine-tuning changes the model's inner representations. With a small set of given calibration prompts, the hypernetwork maps these fingerprints to the parameters of the \ssn{} in a single forward pass. The generated \ssn{} runs alongside the frozen fine-tuned model and performs prompt-level safety classification: harmful prompts are routed to refusal, while safe prompts are answered by the original fine-tuned model. Thus, HyperSafe requires no gradient updates, no safety data at deployment time, and no modification to the deployed model weights. We evaluate HyperSafe on two model families, Qwen2-7B and LLaMA-3-8B, across multiple safety benchmarks. HyperSafe reduces harmful response rates from 19-31% to below 1% on every held-out checkpoint, while keeping downstream task accuracy within 1% of the fine-tuned baseline on average. Code is available at https://github.com/nokronim/project-safety-remedy.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: training-free prompt-level safety classifier alongside frozen LLM
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 无需训练的提示级安全分类器与冻结LLM并行运行
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 无需训练的提示级安全分类器与冻结LLM并行运行
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 无需训练的提示级安全分类器与冻结LLM并行运行
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: HyperSafe restores safety in fine-tuned LLMs by generating a model-specific Safe Side Network via a hypernetwork, performing prompt-level safety classification without gradient updates or weight modification, reducing harmful responses to below 1% while preserving task accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对微调后LLM安全对齐易受损的问题，HyperSafe提出一种推理时安全恢复框架，通过超网络生成模型专属的安全侧网络（SSN），对有害提示进行分类并路由至拒绝响应，无需梯度更新或修改部署模型权重。实验表明该方法将有害响应率降至1%以下，同时保持下游任务精度损失在1%以内，为微调模型安全防护提供了非侵入式方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对微调后LLM安全对齐易受损的问题，HyperSafe提出一种推理时安全恢复框架，通过超网络生成模型专属的安全侧网络（SSN），对有害提示进行分类并路由至拒绝响应，无需梯度更新或修改部署模型权重。实验表明该方法将有害响应率降至1%以下，同时保持下游任务精度损失在1%以内，为微调模型安全防护提供了非侵入式方案。

quick_skim.title: Securing LLMs in the Wild: Privacy and Security Challenges at the Edge
quick_skim.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are rapidly moving from research settings into the wild, deployed on enterprise infrastructure, personal devices, and edge platforms. While cloud deployments offer scalable compute, concerns over data sovereignty, compliance, latency, and third-party dependence are driving organizations toward edge and on-premise LLMs. This shift introduces new security and privacy challenges: limited compute and memory force aggressive optimizations, including quantization, pruning, model partitioning, and parameter-efficient adaptation, each of which can introduce vulnerabilities and reshape the threat landscape. We describe this tension as the Security-Efficiency Paradox, mechanisms that improve efficiency may weaken robustness, expose new attack surfaces, or increase privacy risks. We examine how compression can degrade safety alignment, how partitioned inference enables reconstruction attacks, and how continuous local adaptation may cause privacy leakage and model drift. To analyze these risks, we introduce a deployment-centric taxonomy organized around three architectural constraints: the Memory Wall, the Quadratic Wall, and the Compute Wall. We derive a unified constraint model that quantifies when unsafe optimizations become unavoidable, linking each wall to specific attack surfaces. Building on this model, we propose the Secure Operational Efficiency Score (SOES), a holistic metric balancing task accuracy, jailbreak resistance, and privacy against energy, memory, and latency, enabling practitioners to configure edge LLMs under real-world hardware limits. We further present a practical decision procedure and targeted mitigations for each optimization-induced vulnerability. Together, these contributions provide a co-designed framework for jointly evaluating security, privacy, and efficiency, laying a foundation for securing edge-native intelligent systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: analyzing security-privacy risks of quantization, pruning, and parameter-efficient adaptation for edge LLM deployment
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分析量化、剪枝和参数高效适配在边缘LLM部署中的安全隐私风险
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分析量化、剪枝和参数高效适配在边缘LLM部署中的安全隐私风险
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分析量化、剪枝和参数高效适配在边缘LLM部署中的安全隐私风险
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Identifies the Security-Efficiency Paradox for edge LLMs where quantization, pruning, and parameter-efficient adaptation may weaken robustness, and proposes SOES metric and decision procedure for balancing accuracy, safety, and efficiency under hardware limits.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 边缘部署LLM时，量化、剪枝和参数高效适配等效率优化可能削弱安全鲁棒性，形成安全-效率悖论。本文提出以内存墙、二次墙和计算墙为核心的部署中心分类法，推导统一约束模型量化不安全优化何时不可避免，并提出SOES指标在硬件限制下平衡任务准确率、越狱抵抗和隐私与能耗、内存和延迟。同时给出针对每种优化诱导漏洞的实用决策程序和缓解措施，为安全高效边缘智能系统奠定基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 边缘部署LLM时，量化、剪枝和参数高效适配等效率优化可能削弱安全鲁棒性，形成安全-效率悖论。本文提出以内存墙、二次墙和计算墙为核心的部署中心分类法，推导统一约束模型量化不安全优化何时不可避免，并提出SOES指标在硬件限制下平衡任务准确率、越狱抵抗和隐私与能耗、内存和延迟。同时给出针对每种优化诱导漏洞的实用决策程序和缓解措施，为安全高效边缘智能系统奠定基础。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: on-device edge deployable small model for harmful content detection and LLM safety
quick_skim.quality_gate_reason_cn: 动机具体明确，属于框架/分析型工作无实验数据依赖；论文直接讨论量化剪枝对LLM安全的影响及边缘部署，与需求8和11高度相关

quick_skim.title: PVDetector: Detecting Prompt Injection Attacks on Purpose-Specific LLM Agents through Policy-Violation Concept Analysis
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as purpose-specific agents to handle domain-specific tasks such as customer service and code generation. These agents are expected to comply with not only generic safety guardrails but also purpose-specific restrictions tailored to their designated roles. Such additional restrictions enlarge the attack surface, particularly to prompt injection (PI) attacks. To defend against such attacks, existing detection methods primarily rely on analyzing input-output patterns, yet yield limited effectiveness. To address this limitation, we turn to analyzing the hidden activation space and discover that LLMs inherently retain latent policy-violation (PV) concepts when prompted with requests beyond their designated purpose. Particularly, PV concepts capture the semantics of conflicts between user queries and predefined restrictions, implicitly reflecting LLMs' intrinsic awareness of recognizing policy violations. Building on this insight, we propose PVDetector, a training-free framework that detects PI attacks during LLM inference by measuring hidden-state alignment with PV concepts, which are derived offline from the contrastive pairs of policy-violating and policy-compliant prompts. Experiments across multiple LLMs and datasets show that PVDetector achieves <1\% false negative rate with minimal auxiliary overhead, consistently outperforming state-of-the-art methods. Our code is available at https://github.com/Claresigle/PVDetector .
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Training-free framework detecting PI attacks via hidden-state alignment with policy-violation concepts
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 无需训练框架，通过隐状态与策略违规概念对齐检测提示注入攻击
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 无需训练框架，通过隐状态与策略违规概念对齐检测提示注入攻击
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 无需训练框架，通过隐状态与策略违规概念对齐检测提示注入攻击
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Existing prompt injection detection relies on input-output patterns with limited effectiveness. PVDetector is a training-free framework that detects attacks by measuring hidden-state alignment with offline-derived policy-violation concepts, achieving <1% FNR with minimal overhead.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有提示注入检测方法依赖输入输出模式分析，效果有限。PVDetector提出一种无需训练的框架，通过测量隐状态与离线提取的策略违规概念对齐度来检测攻击，在多个LLM和数据集上实现低于1%的漏报率且辅助开销极小，持续优于现有方法。该工作为LLM输入安全提供了一种轻量级无需训练的防护方案。

quick_skim.title: Data-Efficient Adaptation of LLMs via Attention Head Reweighting
quick_skim.abstract: Learning effectively from limited data is critical in domains like security where labeled examples are scarce. Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated some capabilities for data-efficient learning, especially through parameter-efficient adaptation methods, but continue to struggle when faced with few samples for difficult tasks. To meet this challenge, we propose Attention Head Reweighting (AHR), a data-efficient method that adapts LLMs to new text-classification tasks by learning only a single scalar per attention head. This drastically reduces the number of parameters that need to be learned by making use of the functional specialization of individual attention heads. Experiments on diverse open-source text classification datasets show that AHR can outperform standard baselines like LoRA when learning from limited samples, despite having 200-1000x fewer trainable parameters, as our AHR only modifies ~0.0001% of the model's parameters. In addition, our learned weights are easy to interpret and can be analyzed to better understand the mechanisms and attention heads responsible for in-context learning abilities in LLMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Parameter-efficient LLM adaptation via single scalar per attention head for text classification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过每个注意力头学习单一标量实现参数高效LLM文本分类适配
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过每个注意力头学习单一标量实现参数高效LLM文本分类适配
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过每个注意力头学习单一标量实现参数高效LLM文本分类适配
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LLMs struggle with few-sample text classification tasks. This paper proposes Attention Head Reweighting (AHR), learning only one scalar per attention head (~0.0001% parameters), outperforming LoRA with 200-1000x fewer trainable parameters on diverse open-source text classification datasets.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: LLM在少样本文本分类任务上表现不佳。该文提出注意力头重加权方法(AHR)，仅学习每个注意力头的单一标量参数，可训练参数仅占模型0.0001%，比LoRA少200-1000倍。在多种开源文本分类数据集上，AHR在少样本场景下超越LoRA等基线，且学习权重可解释，为参数高效安全分类器提供了可迁移的方法基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: LLM在少样本文本分类任务上表现不佳。该文提出注意力头重加权方法(AHR)，仅学习每个注意力头的单一标量参数，可训练参数仅占模型0.0001%，比LoRA少200-1000倍。在多种开源文本分类数据集上，AHR在少样本场景下超越LoRA等基线，且学习权重可解释，为参数高效安全分类器提供了可迁移的方法基础。

quick_skim.title: Reliability Scaling Laws for Quantized Large Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Quantization is a powerful strategy to build capable and resource-efficient large language models (LLMs) by reducing the bitwidth of the parameters. While quantized LLMs achieve state-of-the-art performance on unperturbed inputs using standard predictive metrics, their performance on perturbed inputs, measured using reliability metrics, remains underexplored, despite its importance for reliable deployment. To address this gap, we first conduct a comprehensive reliability evaluation of quantized LLMs consisting of three key components: (1) Uncertainty: We assess the trustworthiness of LLMs quantized to 2, 3, 4, and 8 bits using six different quantization methods, employing established uncertainty metrics. (2) Calibration: We assess how well-calibrated the uncertainty estimates of quantized models are across model scales and bit precisions. (3) Robustness: We design character-level and word-level input perturbations to evaluate the reliability of quantized models under semantically-preserving variations in the inputs that arise in real-world applications. Second, we characterize how reliability scales with the total number of model bits. Our study reveals that while the performance scales monotonically with the total number of bits, the reliability scalings are nonlinear. A reliability peak occurs for 4-bit quantized models, indicating that quantizing moderately sized models offers the best reliability-efficiency trade-off. Additionally, our empirical findings reveal that quantization enhances the robustness of LLMs to natural input perturbations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: comprehensive reliability evaluation of quantized LLMs at 2-8 bits for deployment trade-offs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 对2-8位量化LLM的可靠性进行全面评估以指导部署权衡
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 对2-8位量化LLM的可靠性进行全面评估以指导部署权衡
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 对2-8位量化LLM的可靠性进行全面评估以指导部署权衡
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Quantized LLMs are efficient but their reliability on perturbed inputs is underexplored. This study evaluates uncertainty, calibration, and robustness of LLMs quantized to 2-8 bits, finding that 4-bit models offer the best reliability-efficiency trade-off and quantization can enhance robustness to natural input perturbations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 量化是构建高效大语言模型的重要策略，但量化模型在扰动输入下的可靠性尚未充分探索。本文对2至8位量化的LLM进行了不确定性、校准和鲁棒性三方面的综合评估，并刻画了可靠性与总比特数的缩放规律。研究发现4位量化模型在可靠性与效率间达到最佳权衡，且量化反而增强了模型对自然输入扰动的鲁棒性，为安全敏感场景下的量化部署提供了重要参考。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 量化是构建高效大语言模型的重要策略，但量化模型在扰动输入下的可靠性尚未充分探索。本文对2至8位量化的LLM进行了不确定性、校准和鲁棒性三方面的综合评估，并刻画了可靠性与总比特数的缩放规律。研究发现4位量化模型在可靠性与效率间达到最佳权衡，且量化反而增强了模型对自然输入扰动的鲁棒性，为安全敏感场景下的量化部署提供了重要参考。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: quantized low-bit safety classifier or guard model for LLM content moderation

quick_skim.title: MemoGuard: An Adaptive Runtime for Guarding Against Memory Traps in Communication-Limited Robot Navigation
quick_skim.abstract: Communication-limited robots in mission-critical scenarios such as disaster inspection and search-and-rescue must make reliable onboard decisions without access to remote operators or high-capacity reasoning services. Episodic memory reuse is an attractive low-cost fallback, but retrieval similarity does not guarantee execution validity, i.e., a retrieved action may match the current context yet be unsafe due to changed topology, insufficient battery margin, or unreliable prior outcomes. We call such high-similarity but execution-invalid episodes memory traps. This creates a safety-efficiency design space where similarity only reuse minimizes fallback cost but can be unsafe, while always invoking local reasoning improves safety at high computational and energy cost. This paper presents MemoGuard, a lightweight adaptive runtime that validates episodic memories against topology, resource, and outcome contracts before reuse, invoking fallback only when validation fails. In a graph-based corridor-inspection simulator, MemoGuard reduces battery safety violations by 76.6% over similarity-only top-1 reuse while reducing fallback calls by 21.4% over always reasoning. On an NVIDIA Jetson AGX Xavier with local llama3.2:3b fallback reasoning, this corresponds to 3.67 s and 36.97 J of avoided fallback-reasoning overhead per trial. We open-source MemoGuard at https://github.com/hetheiin/memoguard.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: lightweight adaptive runtime validates low-cost memory reuse and escalates to local LLM only on failure, with edge device latency and energy measurements
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 轻量自适应运行时验证低成本记忆复用，仅在失败时升级至本地LLM，并报告边缘设备延迟与能耗
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 轻量自适应运行时验证低成本记忆复用，仅在失败时升级至本地LLM，并报告边缘设备延迟与能耗
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 轻量自适应运行时验证低成本记忆复用，仅在失败时升级至本地LLM，并报告边缘设备延迟与能耗
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Communication-limited robots face safety-efficiency trade-offs between cheap episodic memory reuse and costly local reasoning. MemoGuard validates memories against contracts before reuse, escalating to local LLM reasoning only when validation fails, reducing safety violations by 76.6% and fallback calls by 21.4% on Jetson AGX Xavier.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 通信受限机器人在低成本情景记忆复用与高开销本地推理之间面临安全与效率权衡。MemoGuard提出轻量自适应运行时，在复用前依据拓扑、资源和结果契约验证记忆，仅在验证失败时升级调用本地llama3.2:3b推理。实验表明，在Jetson AGX Xavier上，相比仅相似度复用，安全违规降低76.6%，相比始终推理，回退调用减少21.4%，每次试验节省3.67秒和36.97焦耳。该工作为端侧级联安全防护提供了可部署方案与资源开销度量。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 通信受限机器人在低成本情景记忆复用与高开销本地推理之间面临安全与效率权衡。MemoGuard提出轻量自适应运行时，在复用前依据拓扑、资源和结果契约验证记忆，仅在验证失败时升级调用本地llama3.2:3b推理。实验表明，在Jetson AGX Xavier上，相比仅相似度复用，安全违规降低76.6%，相比始终推理，回退调用减少21.4%，每次试验节省3.67秒和36.97焦耳。该工作为端侧级联安全防护提供了可部署方案与资源开销度量。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Find cascaded moderation systems where a small model handles common cases and escalates uncertain cases to a larger model
quick_skim.quality_gate_reason_cn: 宽松收录：公开证据没有明确说明数据集或基准可公开获取

quick_skim.title: Conditional Reliability of Toxicity Signals for Multilingual and Code-Mixed Abuse Detection
quick_skim.abstract: Moderation systems increasingly rely on external toxicity tools, but those tools are unreliable under code-mixing, transliteration, slang, and language mismatch. We study the \emph{conditional reliability} of toxicity priors in Indian multilingual and code-mixed short text: English toxicity, Indic abuse, and rule-based severity cues can be useful evidence, but only in some linguistic and abuse-severity contexts. We propose ToxGate, a trust-fusion head that conditions each auxiliary signal on the encoder representation before adding it to the prediction state. Across three short-text abuse datasets, four transformer encoders, and five seeds per setting, ToxGate improves over matched plain encoders in 10 of 12 in-domain settings and 7 of 8 transfer settings. The largest and most interpretable gains occur in high-risk moderation slices, including explicit slurs, violent threats, and cross-dataset transfer. The broader lesson is that moderation systems should treat external toxicity tools and priors as conditional evidence rather than fixed features or ground truth, in focused ablations, source-specific gating gives the strongest results in transfer, severe-abuse slices, and high-risk triage.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: ToxGate trust-fusion head for conditional toxicity signal gating in abuse detection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: ToxGate信任融合头用于滥用检测中的条件性毒性信号门控
quick_skim.llm_evidence: ToxGate信任融合头用于滥用检测中的条件性毒性信号门控
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: ToxGate信任融合头用于滥用检测中的条件性毒性信号门控
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: External toxicity tools are unreliable under code-mixing and language mismatch. ToxGate conditions each auxiliary toxicity signal on the encoder representation before fusion, improving abuse detection in 10/12 in-domain and 7/8 transfer settings, with largest gains in high-risk slices.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有审核系统依赖的外部毒性工具在代码混合和语言不匹配情况下不可靠。ToxGate提出一种信任融合头，在融合前将每个辅助毒性信号以编码器表示为条件进行门控，在12项域内设置中10项和8项迁移设置中7项优于纯编码器，最高增益出现在高风险审核切片如暴力威胁中。该工作为多语言内容审核提供了轻量级条件信号融合方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有审核系统依赖的外部毒性工具在代码混合和语言不匹配情况下不可靠。ToxGate提出一种信任融合头，在融合前将每个辅助毒性信号以编码器表示为条件进行门控，在12项域内设置中10项和8项迁移设置中7项优于纯编码器，最高增益出现在高风险审核切片如暴力威胁中。该工作为多语言内容审核提供了轻量级条件信号融合方案。
